formulas.name: Converse of a Statement
formulas.notes: Used to express that the truth of q implies the truth of p.
formulas.formula: If p implies q, then p is a consequence of q.
formulas.variables: p: the first statement, q: the second statement

formulas.name: Inverse of a Statement
formulas.notes: Used to express that the falsity of q implies the falsity of p.
formulas.formula: If p implies q, then not p implies not q.
formulas.variables: p: the first statement, q: the second statement

formulas.name: Contrapositive of a Statement
formulas.notes: Used to express that the falsity of q implies the falsity of p, which is equivalent to p implying q.
formulas.formula: If not q implies not p, then p implies q.
formulas.variables: p: the first statement, q: the second statement

formulas.name: Addition Principle for Counting
formulas.notes: Used to calculate the total number of outcomes when events are counted separately.
formulas.formula: Total number of outcomes = number of outcomes of one event + number of outcomes of another event - number of outcomes of both events.
formulas.variables: Total number of outcomes: T, number of outcomes of one event: n(A), number of outcomes of another event: n(B), number of outcomes of both events: n(A ∩ B)

formulas.name: Multiplication Principle for Counting
formulas.notes: Used to calculate the total number of outcomes when events are counted together.
formulas.formula: Total number of outcomes = number of outcomes of first event × number of outcomes of second event.
formulas.variables: Total number of outcomes: T, number of outcomes of first event: n(A), number of outcomes of second event: n(B)

formulas.name: Principle of Inclusion-Exclusion
formulas.notes: Used to calculate the total number of outcomes in the union of two events.
formulas.formula: n(A ∪ B) = n(A) + n(B) - n(A ∩ B)
formulas.variables: n(A ∪ B): total number of outcomes, n(A): number of outcomes of first event, n(B): number of outcomes of second event, n(A ∩ B): number of outcomes of both events

formulas.name: De Morgan's Law
formulas.notes: Used to express the complement of the union and intersection of sets.
formulas.variables: A': complement of set A, B': complement of set B, A ∩ B: intersection of sets A and B, A ∪ B: union of sets A and B

formulas.name: Laws of Complement
formulas.notes: Used to express the properties of the complement of a set.
formulas.variables: A': complement of set A, U: universal set, ∅: empty set, n(A ∪ A'): total number of outcomes in the union of set A and its complement

formulas.name: Complement Law
formulas.notes: Used to calculate the number of outcomes in the complement of a set.
formulas.variables: n(A'): number of outcomes in the complement of set A, n(U): total number of outcomes in the universal set, n(A): number of outcomes in set A

formulas.name: Principle of Mathematical Induction
formulas.notes: Used to prove that a statement is true for all positive integers.
formulas.formula: P(1) is true, and if P(k) is true for any positive integer k, then P(k + 1) is true.
formulas.variables: P(k): a statement to be proved for all positive integers k
